subject: [ARCH-REVIEWED] ARCH-IT-ARCH-v9 iteration 1

critical_issues.description: architecture.md is 213 bytes — NOT an architecture specification. It's a meta-document noting 'Sai-authored stand-in due to architect Hermes producing 320K prose without tool calls'. No system design, components, data flows, or interfaces defined.
critical_issues.fix_required: Write actual architecture.md with: problem statement, system overview, component definitions, data flows, interface specifications, security model. Must be >5KB.

critical_issues.description: Architecture doesn't state WHAT it's trying to solve. Is this documenting v7.3? Improving the pipeline? What are actual requirements?
critical_issues.fix_required: Explicit requirements: 'This architecture specifies v7.3 of the Karios Migration Pipeline which does X' with specific functional requirements.

critical_issues.description: RESILIENCE=FAIL. No circuit breakers defined. EC-7 (Telegram 409 Conflict) shows long-poll causes issues, but no circuit breaker specified. EC-9 (Devops build failure) is 'to address in next iter' — unhandled.
critical_issues.fix_required: Add circuit breaker spec for Telegram (max 1 req/3s), Redis (connection pool limits), and each agent (task timeout). Handle EC-9 Devops build failure.

critical_issues.description: No timeout values defined anywhere. If any operation hangs, pipeline stalls indefinitely.
critical_issues.fix_required: Define timeout for: agent task execution (max 600s), Redis operations (max 30s), Telegram API calls (max 10s), gap state transitions (max 60s).

critical_issues.description: No resource limits defined for any agent. Redis streams could grow unbounded (PEL entries). Vault could grow unbounded.
critical_issues.fix_required: Define MemoryLimit, CPUQuota for all 9 systemd services. Define Redis stream MAXLEN and entry TTL. Define vault cleanup policy (TTL on entries).

critical_issues.description: EC-9 (Devops build failure) has no handler. Gap state could be lost if orchestrator crashes mid-update. No transaction semantics for state changes.
critical_issues.fix_required: Define Devops build failure handler in orchestrator. Define transaction semantics for gap state updates (WATCH/MULTI/EXEC or equivalent).

critical_issues.description: No component model defined. Who are the 9 agents? What does each own? What are their interfaces? What happens when each fails?
critical_issues.fix_required: Define all 9 agents with: responsibilities, inputs, outputs, failure modes, restart behavior. Document in architecture.md.

critical_issues.description: EC-9 is explicitly unhandled. Devops build failure has no handler — marked 'to address in next iter'. This is a known failure mode with no recovery.
critical_issues.fix_required: Implement Devops build failure handler: retry once, then escalate to Sai via Telegram with full context.

reasoning: architecture.md is 213 bytes — a meta-document noting architect failure, NOT an architecture specification. Documents describe SYMPTOMS of past failures and pipeline validation tests, but no actual system design. RESILIENCE=FAIL: no circuit breakers, no timeout budgets, no resource limits, EC-9 unhandled. Pattern matches ARCH-IT-ARCH-v6 and v7 failures — architect keeps producing empty/incomplete architecture.md for meta-architecture gaps.
self_reflection_summary: No adjustment needed — this is a clear REJECT. Pattern is clear: architect produces empty architecture.md for ARCH-IT-ARCH gaps. Orchestrator should not dispatch ARCH-IT-ARCH gaps to architect — Sai should handle meta-architecture directly.
